boundaries: Keep planning focused on VM-21 only.
Do not widen into VM-20 during this wave.
Keep VM-22 out of scope for this run unless separately approved.
Keep VM-30, VM-31, VM-G, VM-C, and VM-M references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep stale, superseded, repealed, companion, or implementation guidance as notes only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.

topicMap.label: Background and principles
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the background material review-only until the scope and effective-date slice is captured.

topicMap.label: Scope and effective date
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the applicability and phase-in language separate from reserve mechanics.

topicMap.label: Reserve methodology and aggregate reserve structure
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the aggregate-reserve rule separate from the later SR projection mechanics.

topicMap.label: SR projection entry
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the accumulated-deficiency projection and starting-asset context together, but stop before the later asset mechanics.

topicMap.label: SR asset and iteration mechanics
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep NAER, direct iteration, annuitization, and ASOP context review-only.

topicMap.label: Reinsurance ceded and Section 6 opener
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the reinsurance opener separate from the prescribed projection details.

topicMap.label: Section 6 assumptions and cohort mechanics
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the expense, withdrawal, and cohort assumptions narrow and review-only.

topicMap.label: Section 6 ITM tables and look-ups
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the ITM tables and factor look-ups separate from Section 7.

topicMap.label: Alternative methodology general layer
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the general alternative-methodology rule and the VM-C cross-reference review-only.

topicMap.label: Alternative methodology tables and product characteristics
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the GC factor tables, asset-class tables, and product-characteristic look-ups separate from Section 8.

topicMap.label: Scenario generation
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the stochastic scenario-generation opener separate from the hedging section.

topicMap.label: Hedging under a future non-index credit hedging strategy
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep hedge strategy, error-factor, and documentation language review-only.

topicMap.label: Contract holder behavior assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep behavior assumptions and sensitivity concepts narrow and review-only.

topicMap.label: Prudent estimate mortality assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep mortality credibility, improvement, and margin language separate from later assumption guidance.

topicMap.label: Other guidance and requirements for assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the margin, sensitivity, and expense cleanup layer review-only.

topicMap.label: Allocation of aggregate reserves to the contract level
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the closing boundary explicit so VM-22 stays out of scope.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and chapter context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat terms and term-boundary clarifications as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat reserve-method organization, aggregation, and reserve-floor language as structural method content.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat formulas, formula steps, and calculation ordering as calculation structure.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Keep formulas with their setup terms, defined variables, and immediate explanatory context.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat prescribed assumption values or formula inputs as fixed-source assumptions.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat prudent-estimate or company-selected assumptions as review-only until the actuarial basis is clear.
reviewStandards.scenarioOrStochasticRequirement: Treat scenario generation, stochastic projections, and simulation rules as stochastic requirements.
reviewStandards.assetModelingJudgment: Treat asset mix, investment strategy, hedge selection, and proxy mapping as human judgment unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.hedgingOrRiskMitigation: Treat hedging, derivative, and risk-mitigation language as review-only unless the section fully spells out the rule.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing, submission, report, and opinion requirements as reporting requirements.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat retention, memorandum, support-file, and evidence language as documentation expectations.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to other VM sections, models, or appendices as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat introductions, examples, navigation text, and chapter background as background content unless it imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat boundary slices and transition pages as review-only windows that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat assumptions, margins, reserve interpretations, and boundary cases as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.staleSupersededCompanionGuidance: Treat stale, superseded, companion, or implementation guidance as review-only support and do not promote it.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The VM-21 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The VM-21 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Chapter-window checks for the confirmed VM-21 page range 143-225.
Cross-reference flag coverage for VM-20, VM-C, VM-G, VM-30, VM-31, and VM-M.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Batch-definition synchronization once VM-21 extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/vm21-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-022 onward sequence when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the VM-21 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch IDs.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the VM-21 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the VM-22 exclusion.
npm run vm21:batch should use the shared runner and continue to honor the same stop conditions as the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled VM-21 planning artifact. Planned batches 022-037 remain review-only by default and are not extraction runs.
